reasoning:
On February 20, the market was pricing the probability of a ceasefire within 90 days (by April 19, 2025) at around 25%. This was driven by reports of a US-Russia drafted 3-stage peace plan and rumors of a ceasefire target date of April 20 (Easter). However, on February 28, negotiations effectively collapsed during a highly publicized Oval Office meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy. Zelenskyy emphatically rejected an immediate ceasefire without security guarantees, pointing to Russia's history of violating previous truces. Trump responded combatively, threatening to cut off aid and telling Zelenskyy to return "when he's ready for peace." Zelenskyy left the meeting early, and a planned press conference was cancelled. 

Given this massive negative diplomatic rupture and the short 48-day window remaining until the April 19 deadline, the probability of both Ukraine and Russia officially announcing a mutually agreed ceasefire across all theaters is exceedingly low. Even facing an aid cutoff, capitulating and finalizing a formal agreement within under 7 weeks is highly unlikely due to the deep mistrust and logistical hurdles involved. I estimate the probability has dropped significantly from the Feb 20 baseline, placing it at 6%.
